1.title: An apostle by Christ, not by men
1.text: Paul opens the letter by defending his apostolic authority: it did not come from men, but directly from Jesus Christ and God the Father. This point is central to everything he will say in Galatians.

1.question: According to verse 4, why did Christ give himself?
options: For our sins, to deliver us from this present evil world

1.template: But though we, or an {0} from heaven, preach any other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him be accursed.

1.question: How does Paul say he received the gospel he preaches?
options: By learning it from the other apostles
By the revelation of Jesus Christ
From the traditions of his fathers
By reading the scriptures of the Jews

1.title: From persecutor to preacher
1.text: Before his conversion, Paul (Saul) fiercely persecuted the church of God. Here he recalls that past to show that only divine intervention could have turned him into an apostle.

1.template: Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into {0}, and returned again unto Damascus.

pairs.right: Region where the recipient churches were
Curse upon anyone who preaches another gospel

2.template: I went up again to {0} with Barnabas

2.title: Titus the Greek
2.text: Titus was a Christian of Greek (Gentile) background. That he was not compelled to be circumcised became living proof that Gentiles were accepted through faith, without the law of Moses.

2.question: Who were regarded as "pillars" and gave Paul the right hands of fellowship?
Where did Paul withstand Peter to the face because he was to be blamed?

pairs.right: Comes by faith in Jesus Christ
What the Judaizers demanded of Gentiles

2.template: I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but {0} liveth in me

3.title: The Galatians
3.text: Galatia was a region in central Asia Minor (modern Turkey). Paul writes with strong emotion, calling the believers 'foolish' for abandoning the gospel of faith they had received.

3.question: According to Paul, how did the Galatians receive the Spirit?
options: By the works of the law

3.template: Even as {0} believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.

pairs.right: The means by which the just shall live
Brings a curse on those who do not keep it all
Redeemed us from the curse of the law

3.question: Whom does Paul identify as the 'seed' promised to Abraham?

3.title: What is the law for?
3.text: Paul teaches that the law of Moses was added because of transgressions, as a temporary measure, until Christ should come, the one to whom the promise was made.

3.question: Is the law against the promises of God?

3.template: Wherefore the law was our {0} to bring us unto Christ, that we might be justified by faith.

3.question: According to verse 28, what is true of those who are in Christ Jesus?
options: They are divided by nation and class
They are all one in Christ Jesus

4.template: But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his {0}, made of a woman, made under the law,

4.title: "Abba, Father"
4.text: "Abba" is an intimate Aramaic word for "father," much like "daddy." Paul keeps it in its original form to show the affectionate closeness the Spirit creates between adopted sons and God.

4.question: According to verse 7, what are we through Christ, being no longer servants?
What does Paul lament that the Galatians are observing?
options: Days, and months, and times, and years

4.question: How does Paul say he first preached the gospel to the Galatians?

4.template: but received me as an {0} of God, even as Christ Jesus.

4.question: According to the scripture Paul cites, who were the mothers of Abraham's two sons?

4.template: Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are the children of {0}.

5.title: The yoke of bondage
5.text: A "yoke" was the wooden bar placed on oxen for labor. Paul uses this image to describe the attempt to bind Christians to keep the law of Moses as a condition of salvation.

5.question: According to Paul, what would happen to those who were circumcised seeking justification by the law?
options: They would be exempt from the law
They would inherit the kingdom at once

5.template: A little {0} leaveneth the whole lump.

5.question: In one word, in what is all the law fulfilled?
options: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself
Thou shalt not touch the unclean

5.template: Walk in the {0}, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh.

5.question: According to verse 18, those led by the Spirit are not under what?

pairs.left: Against the fruit of the Spirit
Those who do the works of the flesh
pairs.right: Shall not inherit the kingdom of God

5.title: Crucifying the flesh
5.text: Paul says those who are Christ's have "crucified the flesh," an image of the willing death of sinful desires in order to live by the Spirit.

pairs.left: Restore in the spirit of meekness
pairs.right: Help one overtaken in a fault

6.template: Bear ye one another’s burdens, and so fulfil the law of {0}.

6.title: Two different burdens
6.text: Verses 2 and 5 seem to contradict, but they use distinct ideas: we are to help carry one another's heavy loads (v. 2), yet each person still answers for his own personal responsibility before God (v. 5).

6.question: According to verse 7, what happens to whatsoever a man soweth?

6.template: but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap {0} everlasting.

6.title: Be not weary in well doing
6.text: The imagery of sowing and reaping (vv. 7–9) teaches patience: just as a farmer waits for the right season, the Saints reap the fruit of righteousness "in due season," if they faint not.

6.question: What does Paul point out in verse 11 about the letter?
options: That he wrote it in large letters with his own hand
That it was written by a scribe
That it was his first letter
That it was translated from Hebrew

6.template: But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus {0}

6.question: According to verse 15, what counts in Christ Jesus?

6.template: From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the {0} of the Lord Jesus.
